name: Block package performance and allocation reduction

description: Baseline - current block production performance with 100 txs

asi.hotspots: sha256.New() in leafHashOpt (~213B), txsToByteSlices allocs [][]byte, Data.ToProto allocs, DACommitment creates pruned Data, ApplyBlock converts Txs to [][]byte

description: sync.Pool for sha256.Hash in leafHashOpt — eliminates 2 sha256.New() allocations per block

asi.hypothesis: pool sha256.Hash to avoid 213B allocation per sha256.New()
asi.next_target: Data.ToProto allocates [][]byte via txsToByteSlices — should be biggest remaining win

description: Unsafe reinterpret cast of Txs to [][]byte in ApplyBlock — eliminates make([][]byte, n) allocation

asi.hypothesis: use unsafe.Slice/unsafe.SliceData instead of make+loop for Txs→[][]byte conversion
asi.note: types.Tx = []byte so types.Txs = []Tx has identical memory layout to [][]byte

description: Direct pb.Data serialization in DACommitment — avoids pruned Data wrapper and txsToByteSlices allocations

asi.empty_txs_safe: unsafe.Slice on 0-len slice is safe in Go 1.21+
asi.hypothesis: Use pb.Data directly with unsafe reinterpreted Txs instead of creating pruned Data wrapper
asi.note: Must still handle empty_txs case - need to check if nil Metadata affects wire format
asi.next_target: txsToByteSlices in Data.ToProto allocates [][]byte; Data.Hash allocates from MarshalBinary

description: unsafe.Slice in Data.ToProto() — eliminates txsToByteSlices [][]byte allocation

asi.hypothesis: Use unsafe.Slice in Data.ToProto() to avoid txsToByteSlices allocation
asi.next_target: Data.Hash() marshal + sha256 allocation; Header.ToProto() allocates pb.Header; Data.Size() marshals for metrics
asi.notes: Biggest single win yet — per-TX allocation eliminated for every protobuf encoding

description: Reverted hand-written HashSlim wire encoder — produced different hashes than MarshalBinary

asi.hypothesis: Direct protobuf wire encoding in HashSlim to avoid pb.Header/pb.Version allocations
asi.result: Hash mismatch — wire encoding differences in version field (0a02 vs 0a04)
asi.rollback_reason: Hand-written encoder produces different byte output than protobuf MarshalBinary; would break hash verification
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on safe optimizations: avoid creating pb.SignedHeader/pb.Signer structs when possible, reduce allocations in store path, look at SignedHeader.ToProto hot path
